knowledge_components.name: Variables represent quantities that function within arithmetic and algebraic rules

complicators.name: Understand single variables as placeholders for numbers in arithmetic contexts, up to two operations
Recognize that variables follow arithmetic properties (commutative associative distributive), up to two variables
Work with multiple variables in expressions

knowledge_components.name: Verbal statements and mathematical relationships can be represented as algebraic expressions

complicators.name: Translate simple phrases (sum, difference, product, quotient)

knowledge_components.name: Expressions are built from terms with distinct roles (coefficients variables constants)

complicators.name: Identify terms coefficients variables and constants
Distinguish between like terms and unlike terms
Understand how coefficients and variables interact within a term

knowledge_components.name: Equivalent expressions can be formed by combining like terms and applying properties

complicators.name: Combine like terms by adding/subtracting coefficients
Apply distributive property to expand/factor expressions
Simplify expressions with multiple variables and coefficients and verify equivalent expressions
